requirement.original: Refactor the /allow bypass system to fix 6 architectural issues identified by the architect. Scope constraint (non-negotiable): the set of operations bypassable via /allow must remain exactly identical to today's.
requirement.clarified: Fix allow-1 through allow-6 as architect described. allow-1: remove dead sid variable. allow-2: centralise allowlist check in dispatcher (push exempt). allow-3: remove 6 dead per-rule callsites in bash hook. allow-4: no code change needed. allow-5: move IS_SUBAGENT assignment to top of bash hook to eliminate duplicate inline parse. allow-6: Won't Have — consolidation not feasible this cycle.
requirement.what: Structural refactoring of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py and pretool-bash-safety.sh to eliminate dead code, add structural enforcement, and remove duplicate computation
requirement.why: 6 architectural issues degrade maintainability, create silent omission risk (allow-2), and cause reader confusion (allow-3). allow-1 is dead code. allow-5 is a duplicate subprocess invocation per bypass check.

included: hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — M1 (dead sid), M2 (central allowlist check)
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — M3 (remove 6 secondary calls), M4 (IS_SUBAGENT relocation)
excluded: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — allow-6 consolidation is Won't Have this cycle

success_criteria: _check_git_allowlist called centrally in dispatcher for all non-push operations; push exempt
5 per-function _check_git_allowlist calls removed from non-push evaluate-* functions
git push --force still blocked with /allow grant present (scope invariant)
6 secondary check_and_consume_allowlist calls removed from pretool-bash-safety.sh
Global short-circuit at line 615 remains unconditional
systemctl non-happy bypass still works via global short-circuit
IS_SUBAGENT assigned once at top of pretool-bash-safety.sh
Inline parse inside check_and_consume_allowlist replaced with $IS_SUBAGENT reference
constraints: Scope invariant: git push --force must remain blocked even with /allow grant
Absolute bans (session_dirs.txt, happy-session-recovery, happy-restart, Layer 1.A-E) must remain unbypassable
Do not touch userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh or posttool-allowlist-consume.py
Global short-circuit at line 615 must remain unconditional and first in the rule chain

path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — read for allow-6 analysis; no change
out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: hooks/lib/allowlist.py — grant-read implementation; allow-6 consolidation deferred

out_of_scope_observations.observation: allow-6: read_grant() has different matching semantics from _check_git_allowlist (tool_name match vs command-string match). Consolidation would require unifying the interface or adding a command-match variant. Feasibility: not safe within a single cycle — bash heredoc in pretool-bash-safety.sh is a complex V1/V2/V3 implementation with SIGALRM timeout + flock + compound-command split; replacing it with a Python subprocess call would require adding a CLI entry point and changing the result-parsing protocol. Deferred to a dedicated future cycle.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: 6 architectural issues in the /allow bypass system: dead variable, unstructured per-function enforcement, dead secondary callsites, confirmed-correct item, duplicate IS_SUBAGENT parse, and 3 independent grant-read implementations
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Incremental additions to the bypass system over multiple cycles without a centralised refactoring pass. Each new evaluate-* function was added with a copy-paste of the _check_git_allowlist call; the global short-circuit in pretool-bash-safety.sh was added later without removing the per-rule calls; IS_SUBAGENT was added at the bottom of the script for a specific use case without reorganising the earlier function that needed the same check.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — not a regression; accumulated architectural debt
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Correct security additions made in isolation under time pressure; no refactoring pass followed
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: allow-2: new evaluate-* functions silently miss the bypass check. allow-3: structural inconsistency confuses readers about whether per-rule calls are load-bearing. allow-5: duplicate subprocess per bypass check (performance + drift risk).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Current state — no regression, pure architectural debt
observations: sid = _get_session_id(data) at pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:658 — assigned, never read in _evaluate_command scope (inferred: static code reading)
_check_git_allowlist calls at lines 455, 480, 583, 602, 615, 648 — 6 per-function calls with no dispatcher-level enforcement (inferred: static code reading)

check_and_consume_allowlist at line 615 fires unconditionally before any block rule; secondary calls at 805, 984, 995, 1007, 1027, 1110 have identical pattern-match semantics with same input — dead code (inferred: static code reading + logic trace)
IS_SUBAGENT assignment at line 1068 is after line 615 where check_and_consume_allowlist is called; inner parse at lines 142-147 is structurally identical — duplicate (inferred: static code reading)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime observation; hook logic is deterministic and the dead-code analysis is provable by static trace)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: static code reading; dead-code claims are verifiable by control-flow trace

attempts.actual_change: Added line 805 call; added _evaluate_push allowlist check at pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:583
attempts.outcome: Cycle closed successfully; this cycle is follow-on architectural cleanup

development_approach.strategy: M1+M2 together in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py (restructure _evaluate_command: remove dead sid, add central allowlist check, remove 5 per-function calls, add push-exemption comment). development_approach.validation_approach: Static inspection: grep confirms removal of dead sid, 5 per-function _check_git_allowlist calls, 6 secondary check_and_consume_allowlist calls, inline IS_SUBAGENT parse. grep confirms IS_SUBAGENT assigned in first 30 lines of bash script. grep confirms _evaluate_push still contains _check_git_allowlist call. Scope invariant: trace _evaluate_push — _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation is still before _check_git_allowlist.

research_findings.reason: Straightforward architectural refactoring of existing security hooks with well-understood code. No new patterns or architectures introduced. Root cause and fix are clear from static code reading.
